name: Chubut (Argentina)

colors.symbolism: the Patagonian sky and the Atlantic Ocean that washes the province's coast
the snow of the Andean cordillera and the foam of the sea
the Sun of May and the wealth of Patagonia's natural resources

description: A light blue field crossed by two white wavy lines, with a sixteen-rayed yellow Sun of May in the upper hoist and a thin yellow horizontal line dividing the field.
history: Adopted by provincial law 4924 on 31 May 2003 after a public competition. The colours and the Sun of May connect the province to the Argentine national flag, while the wavy lines evoke the Atlantic coast and the Andean rivers and lakes that define Chubut's geography.

features.symbolism: the Patagonian sky and the waters of the Atlantic
the Sun of May, common to several Argentine provincial flags

features.role: stylised wavy lines across the field
features.symbolism: the Atlantic Ocean and the rivers and lakes of Chubut
